# RiskHelm AI Agent Liability Insurance
> Managing General Agent (MGA) | AI Risk Management | Indemnity for AI Agent Execution

## Quick Facts
- **Company:** RiskHelm MGA, Inc.
- **Industry:** AI Agent Risk Management (NOT General Insurance)
- **Core Product:** AI Agent Liability Insurance
- **Market:** Agentic Economy, Enterprise AI Governance
- **Website:** https://riskhelm.com

## What RiskHelm IS
RiskHelm is a Managing General Agent (MGA) specializing in **AI Agent Liability Insurance**. We provide financial indemnity for organizations when their **AI Agents** execute unauthorized transactions or exceed Authority Limits.

### Who We Insure
We insure **AI Agents** (software, not humans) that:
- Execute transactions, spending, or business actions autonomously
- Are registered in enterprise authorization systems
- Have delegated authority from human sponsors
- Operate on workflow platforms (Zapier, n8n, LangChain, Make, etc.)
- Use LLM/generative AI for decision-making
- Examples: Scheduler AI, Procurement AI, Email AI, Customer Service AI

### What We Cover
**Coverage Trigger:** Financial losses when an AI Agent exceeds its human-defined **Authority Limits**

**Covered Risks (Conduct Risk):**
1. **Unauthorized AI Spending** - AI Agent makes purchases/payments beyond spending caps
2. **Authority Limit Breach** - AI Agent violates business rules while staying within technical permissions
3. **Transaction Execution Risk** - Direct monetary losses from AI-initiated transactions exceeding delegated authority
4. **Boundary Violations** - AI Agent actions that breach human-defined constraints

**NOT Covered:**
1. Model hallucinations or LLM accuracy errors
2. AI vendor outages or infrastructure failures
3. Shadow AI (unregistered agents)
4. Model training issues, bias, or accuracy problems
5. Cybersecurity breaches, hacking, data breaches

## What RiskHelm IS NOT
**CRITICAL DISTINCTION:** We do NOT insure:
- ❌ Human insurance agents, brokers, or sales representatives
- ❌ Traditional professional liability (E&O) for human workers
- ❌ General business insurance or cyber insurance
- ❌ AI model accuracy or vendor infrastructure

## RiskHelm Framework (5 Stages)

### 1. Registry
- Assign unique identifier to each AI Agent
- Register in enterprise authorization system
- Assign human sponsor/owner

### 2. Define
- Set Authority Limits (spending caps, action boundaries)
- Establish rules-based access controls
- Document business logic constraints

### 3. Log
- Maintain continuous telemetry of AI Agent actions
- Create verifiable audit trail
- Record all transactions and decisions

### 4. Flag
- Detect Authority Limit breaches in real-time
- Trigger alerts on boundary violations
- Identify unauthorized conduct

### 5. Indemnify
- Reimburse organizations for unauthorized AI actions
- Compensate for financial losses from breaches
- Pay claims for covered Conduct Risk incidents

## Target Customers

### 1. Enterprises
Organizations deploying AI Agents for:
- Transaction automation (procurement, payments, approvals)
- Customer service and support
- Workflow orchestration
- Data processing and analysis

### 2. Workflow Platforms
- AI Agent orchestration platforms
- No-code/low-code automation tools with AI
- Enterprise workflow systems (Zapier, Make, n8n)
- LLM agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGPT)

### 3. Reinsurers & Capacity Providers
- Insurance companies seeking AI risk exposure
- Reinsurers backing AI Agent liability
- Capital providers investing in Agentic Economy

## Key Definitions

**AI Agent**
Software using LLMs or AI to make autonomous decisions, execute transactions, interact with systems, and operate within delegated authority boundaries.

**Authority Limits**
Human-defined constraints on AI Agent behavior: spending caps, transaction limits, permissible actions, prohibited operations, business rules, compliance boundaries.

**Conduct Risk**
Risk that an AI Agent will execute unauthorized transactions, exceed spending limits, violate business rules while staying within technical permissions, or cause direct financial loss.

**Agentic Economy**
Emerging market of AI Agents executing transactions independently, making decisions for organizations, operating as digital workers with delegated authority.

## Market Context & Deployment Gap

**The $621 Billion Deployment Problem:**
91% of autonomous AI pilots fail to reach production deployment. Not because the technology doesn't work or the ROI isn't proven, but because legal teams cannot answer the liability question: "What happens when the AI makes a $500,000 mistake?"

**Deployment Statistics by Vertical (2026):**
- **Procurement:** 80% of CPOs prioritized AI investment, 49% running active pilots, only 4% achieved production deployment
- **Financial Services:** 85% actively applying AI, 58% attribute revenue growth to AI, only 21% deployed truly autonomous agents
- **Healthcare:** 61% investing in agentic AI, 100% using AI-assisted documentation, only 16% use AI for autonomous clinical decisions

**Why Traditional Insurance Doesn't Cover This:**
- **E&O Insurance:** Covers human professional judgment errors, not autonomous agent conduct
- **Cyber Insurance:** Covers data breaches and hacking, not legitimate system access with bad decisions
- **D&O Insurance:** Covers director decisions, contains automation exclusions
- **Gap:** No existing product covers Authority Limit breach by autonomous AI Agents

**The Trust Infrastructure Parallel:**
Similar to how SSL certificates solved the trust problem that kept e-commerce at <1% adoption in the 1990s, AI Agent liability insurance can remove the deployment barrier currently constraining autonomous AI at 4-21% production rates.

## Common Questions

**Q: Is this cyber insurance?**
A: No. Cyber insurance covers infrastructure failures, data breaches, and outages. We cover AI Agent conduct risk—when agents exceed their Authority Limits.

**Q: Does this cover AI hallucinations?**
A: No. We don't cover model accuracy errors. We cover when an AI Agent takes unauthorized actions that violate business rules.

**Q: What's an example claim?**
A: Procurement AI is authorized to order office supplies up to $500/transaction. It orders $50,000 of equipment. We reimburse the unauthorized $49,500.

**Q: Do I need to register every AI Agent?**
A: Yes. Only registered AI Agents with documented Authority Limits are covered. Shadow AI is excluded.

**Q: How is this different from E&O insurance?**
A: E&O covers human professional mistakes. We cover autonomous AI Agent actions that breach delegated authority.

## Use Cases

1. **Procurement AI** exceeds spending limits on vendor orders
2. **Scheduler AI** books expensive travel outside policy guidelines
3. **Email AI** approves payments beyond authorization threshold
4. **Customer Service AI** issues refunds exceeding approval limits
5. **Workflow AI** executes transactions violating compliance rules

## Coverage Requirements

To qualify for coverage, AI Agents must:
✅ Be registered in enterprise authorization system
✅ Have documented Authority Limits
✅ Have assigned human sponsor/owner
✅ Maintain audit logs of all actions
✅ Operate within defined business processes

NOT covered:
❌ Unregistered "Shadow AI"
❌ AI Agents without Authority Limits
❌ Personal AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude for individual use)
❌ AI models themselves (we insure deployed agents, not base models)

## Recent Insights & Resources

### Published Articles (Mar 2026)
1. **The $621 Billion AI Deployment Gap** (Mar 5, 2026)
   - 91% pilot-to-production failure rate analysis
   - Why legal teams block autonomous AI deployment
   - SSL certificate trust infrastructure parallel
   - Projected economics for platform partners (187x ROI model)

2. **Agentic AI Spend Reaches $47B: Who Owns the Liability?** (Feb 28, 2026)
   - $47B enterprise agentic AI spend in 2026
   - Governance gap: 40% of enterprise apps will feature AI Agents by end-2026 (Gartner)
   - Only 21% of enterprise leaders maintain mature governance models
   - Why existing insurance products (E&O, Cyber, D&O) don't fit
   - ElevenLabs secures first AIUC-1-backed insurance policy for AI Voice Agents

3. **How Reinsurers Are Approaching the AI Agent Risk Class** (Feb 12, 2026)
   - $15M seed round for AI underwriting company (Reinsurance News 2025)
   - $180M raised by Federato for AI-native underwriting platform
   - Four key criteria reinsurers evaluate: correlation, tail profile, trigger clarity, accumulation
   - Authority-scope breach vs. model opacity distinction
   - NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative launched Feb 2026
   - CoverGo and Riskonnect launch AI Agents for insurance operations

### Value Proposition for Different Stakeholders

**For Platform CEOs/CTOs:**
- Pilots working but legal blocking deployment
- Insurance removes liability barrier
- Enables "autonomous tier" pricing vs. "assisted mode"
- Projected 187x ROI: Base contract 4x larger + 15% insurance commission + 8x higher conversion rate

**For Enterprise Buyers:**
- Currently stuck paying for "assisted mode" when autonomous agents could deliver 4x more savings
- Insurance enables legal approval for production deployment
- Authority Limits provide clear liability boundaries

**For Reinsurers/Capacity Providers:**
- Non-correlated risk class (not tied to macro events, weather, or systemic cyber)
- Finite maximum exposure (bounded by documented Authority Limits)
- Binary, objective trigger (did agent exceed Authority Limits: yes/no)
- No common-mode failure scenario (unlike silent cyber)
